title: Globalisation and identity
summary: 9699 — how global flows reshape national, ethnic, and personal identities.
learningObjectives: Evaluate postmodern and post-colonial views of identity.
Apply concepts to diaspora and transnational communities.

simpleExplanation.title: Global culture, local selves
simpleExplanation.summary: Global media and migration create both shared consumer cultures and new hybrid identities that blend local and global influences.
steps: Hybridity — mixing global and local cultures.

flashcards.back: Dominant nations impose culture on others via media/TNCs.
New ethnic/cultural mixes from migration and global media.
Scattered communities maintaining links to homeland + host society.
Global flows: ethnoscape, mediascape, technoscape, etc.
Global forms reshaped locally — e.g. localised fast food menus.
Resistance to Western definitions; reclaiming indigenous culture.

sections.content: **Globalisation and identity** (7.2) links to **1.3 social identity**. Use **Hall**, **Appadurai**, **Ritzer**.

sections.question: Assess the impact of globalisation on ethnic identities. [15 marks]
sections.solution:
**Homogenisation threat:** Loss of traditional culture; **Western media** defines beauty/lifestyle; youth adopt global consumer identities.

**Hybridity/diaspora:** Hall — **new ethnicities**; dual identities (British-Asian); **transnational** communities online; **creolisation** of culture.

**Resistance:** **Islamic revival**, nationalist movements reclaim identity; **post-colonial** pride.

**Evaluation:** Globalisation **both erodes and recreates** ethnic identity — outcome depends on **power, migration, media access**.

**Conclusion:** Ethnic identity **transformed**, not simply weakened.
